下载PDF摘要：本文未描述工作系统。取而代之的是，它提出了一个关于表示的想法，该想法使几个不同的群体所取得的进步可以组合成一个称为GLOM的假想系统。这些进步包括变压器，神经场，对比表示学习，蒸馏和胶囊。 GLOM回答了这个问题：具有固定体系结构的神经网络如何将图像解析为部分整体的层次结构，而每个部分的结构都具有不同的结构？这个想法只是使用相同向量的孤岛来表示解析树中的节点。如果可以使GLOM正常工作，则当将其应用于视觉或语言时，应极大地提高类变压器系统产生的表示的可解释性

Download PDF  Abstract: This paper does not describe a working system. Instead, it presents a singleidea about representation which allows advances made by several differentgroups to be combined into an imaginary system called GLOM. The advancesinclude transformers, neural fields, contrastive representation learning,distillation and capsules. GLOM answers the question: How can a neural networkwith a fixed architecture parse an image into a part-whole hierarchy which hasa different structure for each image? The idea is simply to use islands ofidentical vectors to represent the nodes in the parse tree. If GLOM can be madeto work, it should significantly improve the interpretability of therepresentations produced by transformer-like systems when applied to vision orlanguage

如果您还没生活在一块石头上，您可能已经听说世界上有多种日期格式，例如美国的日期格式（mm / dd / yyyy）和欧洲的日期格式（dd.mm.yyyy）。如果您足够聪明，那么您可能还注意到，美国佬很无礼，而且很糟糕。一个简单的结论，就是很多人都从中得出结论，欧洲格式是最好的格式，但是我不认为这是真的。如果您是这么想的人之一，那么我在这里（希望）通过向您介绍一种鲜为人知的日期格式ISO 8601来改变主意。
  您可以通过格式名称中的“ ISO”部分看到，这是国际标准化组织（International Organization for Standardization）编写的实际标准。它定义了许多很酷的东西，例如编写时间间隔的方法，这对编写便携式软件很有用，以及日历，其中年份不是按月而是按周分开的，在财务中使用，但是这里我们仅对基础知识感兴趣简化后，核心日期格式如下所示：
  是的，就是这样。您可以完全按照其他日期格式输入年，月，日和时间。没什么特别的，所以您可以在2分钟内学会它。
   这是编写该标准的主要原因，也是人们仍然使用它的原因。其他日期格式可能很难区分。例如，考虑以下日期：
  脱离上下文阅读时，您完全不知道那里发生了什么。这里是02还是月份？除非日期中的日期大于12，否则您就不可能知道一个月：
  这一天的模棱两可是一个非常普遍的问题，通常会在网上出现。人们只是以他们知道的任何日期格式写下日期，甚至没有想到其他人可以用不同的方式解释日期。
 ISO 8601不会出现此问题，因为格式的独特性，它始终很明显是哪一天，哪一天是月份： 
尽管其他日期格式通常不对如何编写内容提供严格的要求，但ISO 8601是一个例外。这里只有一种正确的写日期的方法，这不仅对计算机进行解析很有用，而且对人类避免与其他格式的混淆以及提高可读性也很有帮助。以下是一些限制条件：
 日期中的元素始终用连字符分隔。没有很多日期格式使用此定界符，当在文件名中使用日期时，这也很有用，因为其中通常不接受斜杠。
 元素总是填充到最大位数。这不仅使所有日期看起来都一样漂亮，而且与这种格式的其他怪癖相结合，允许名称中带有日期的文件仅按文件名排序。
 年份始终以完整形式书写。这使得格式在记录时是唯一的，并且消除了它可以采取的任何形式的2000年问题。例如，在写生日时，总是很清楚我们谈论的是哪个世纪。
 时间始终以24小时格式编写，因此不会混淆一天中发生了什么事情。
  乍一看，欧洲格式似乎已逐渐变得不合逻辑-几天变成几个月，几个月变成几年：
  但是，这忽略了数字的一个非常重要的特征-字节序。考虑一个常规数字，例如： 
如您所见，这里的数字与欧洲格式的元素相反。当某物最左边的元素最有价值时，我们称其为大端。
     而且，正如您所看到的，ISO 8601在这方面是完全一致的，因为一切都是大字节序：
  如果考虑时间，情况会变得更糟，因为它是大尾数格式，例如ISO 8601，因此不适用于任何其他日期格式：
 18.12.2002 23:03:59＆lt;-＆lt;-＆lt; ---＆lt;-＆lt;-＆lt;-数字---------＆gt; ＆lt; ------- Elements12 / 18/2002 23：03：59＆lt;-＆lt;-＆lt; ---＆lt;-＆lt;-＆lt;-数字?????????? ＆lt; ------- Elements2002-12-18 23：03：59＆lt; ---＆lt;-＆lt;-＆lt;-＆lt;-＆lt;-数字＆lt; ---------＆lt ; -------元素
 如果您仍然无法在脑海中想像所有这些内容，请查看此Reddit帖子。
  如果您认为ISO 8601是一个愚蠢的事情，在他们的地下室中有人组成，却没人真正使用，请再三考虑，因为事实与事实相去甚远：
 它标准化的事实至少可以说明一些问题。您喜欢的格式是否有整齐的数百页文档，其中有非常详尽的描述，并且得到了国际认可？该标准还远远没有过时-在1988年发布后，它在1991、2000、2004和2019年进行了更新。 
正如我已经提到的那样，该标准在IT中得到了积极的应用，软件所使用的几乎所有内容以及以某种方式涉及书面数字日期格式的内容都已经达到了ISO 8601标准。
 yyyy-mm-dd自一开始就被许多国家（如加拿大，瑞典和日本）改编或用作国家日期格式。有关更多详细信息，请参见本文。
   它没有ISO 8601的任何出色功能，并且在国际上也无法正常工作（即假设您与之交流的人都知道英语）。如果您认为后者不是问题，请想象一下，如果您不得不阅读某人用他们的母语写的一个您不懂的日期，将会有什么样的感觉：
   它对您有用的唯一原因是因为您不习惯它。经过一点练习，它会比您喜欢的日期格式更奇怪。
  首先，有关顺序是小端的说法是很可疑的。您可以确定的唯一情况是，我们谈论的是每天发生的事件，但是我可以想到许多情况，当年份和月份更相关时：
  其次，关联的顺序实际上是不相关的。即使相关性顺序是这种方式，您也不会大声地读出日期，因此无需以某种方式对它们进行排序。如果您对年份不感兴趣，则可以跳过它并阅读日期的结尾，就像在不对小时感兴趣的情况下阅读时间一样。
  我认为，就国际交流（例如在线发布内容）而言，ISO 8601似乎明显优于其他日期格式，并且正如您所看到的，我有足够的理由这么说。尽管这种格式确实吸引了很多观众，但不幸的是，这种格式没有达到预期的规模。通过撰写本文，希望我能使您至少考虑不同的日期格式，并在使人们了解您在说什么日期时更加谨慎。
note_en:
If you haven’t been living under a rock, you’ve probably heard thatthere are different date formats in the world such as the American one(mm/dd/yyyy) and the European one (dd.mm.yyyy). If you’re smartenough, you’ve probably also noticed that the American one makes nosense and is just awful. A simple conclusion that many people draw outof this is that the European format is the best one, however I don’tthink this is true. If you’re one of these people who think so, I’mhere to (hopefully) change your mind by introducing you to alesser-known date format called ISO 8601.
  As you can see by the “ISO” part in the format’s name, it’s an actualstandard written by the International Organization forStandardization. It defines many cool things like a way to write timeintervals, which can be useful for writing portable software, and acalendar where the year is separated not by months but by weeks, whichis used in finances, but here we’re only interested in the basics.Simplified, the core date format looks like this:
  Yup, that’s about it. You write the year, the month, the day, and thenthe time exactly like it’s done in other date formats. There’s nothingextraordinary, so you can learn it in 2 minutes.
   This is the main reason the standard was written and why people stilluse it. Other date formats can be diffucult to tell apart. Forexample, consider this date:
  When you read it out of context, you have absolutely no idea what’sgoing on there. Is  02 here the day or the month? You just can’t knowunless the day in the date is greater than 12, in which case it justcan’t be a month:
  This day-month ambiguity is a really common problem, which oftenoccurs online. People just write down their dates in whatever dateformat they know without even thinking that other people can interpretit in different ways.
 ISO 8601 doesn’t have this problem as it’s always obvious which partis the day and which is the month because of the uniqueness of theformat:
   While other date formats usually don’t provide strict requirements onhow to write something, ISO 8601 is an exception. Here there’s onlyone correct way to write a date, which is not only useful forcomputers to parse, but also helpful for humans to avoid confusionwith other formats and improve readability. Here are some of therestrictions:
 The elements in the date are always separated by a hyphen. Not manydate formats use this delimiter, and this also can be useful whenusing dates inside filenames as slashes are usually not accepted inthem.
 The elements are always padded to the maximum number of digits. Thisnot only makes all of the dates look equally nice, but also, coupledwith other quirks of this format, allows the files with the date inthe name to be sorted just by the filename.
 The year is always written in the full form. This makes the formatunique when written down and eliminates  the year 2000 problem inany forms that it can take. For example, when writing downbirthdays, it always makes it obvious which century we’re talkingabout.
 The time is always written in the 24 hour format, so there can be noconfusion about what half of the day something happened in.
  On the first glance, it seems like the European format is about aslogical as it can get - days go into months and months go into years:
  However, this ignores a very important characteristic of numbers -endianness. Consider a regular number, for example:
  As you can see the digits here do the opposite of what elements do inthe European format. When the leftmost element of something is themost valuable, we call it big endian.
     And, as you can see, ISO 8601 is completely consistent in this regardas everything is big endian:
  The situation becomes even worse if you consider time because it isbig endian, like ISO 8601, thus doesn’t work with any other dateformat:
 18.12.2002 23:03:59<- <- <--- <- <- <- Digits---------> <------- Elements12/18/2002 23:03:59<- <- <--- <- <- <- Digits?????????? <------- Elements2002-12-18 23:03:59<--- <- <- <- <- <- Digits<--------- <------- Elements
 If you’re still having trouble visualizing all of this in your head,look at  this Reddit post.
  If you think that ISO 8601 is a silly thing that someone in theirbasement made up and no one actually uses, think again because thatcan’t be further from the truth:
 The fact that it’s standardized says at least something. Does yourfavorite format has a neat several hundred page document where it’sdescribed in extreme detail and that is internationally recognized?Also the standard is quite far from being dead - after beingpublished in 1988 it was updated in 1991, 2000, 2004, and 2019.
 As I already mentioned, the standard is actively used in IT.Almost everything that is used by software and somehow involves awritten numerical date format already speaks ISO 8601.
 yyyy-mm-dd has been adapted or used since the beginning as anational date format by many countries such as Canada, Sweden, andJapan. See  this article for more details.
   It doesn’t have any of the nice features ISO 8601 has and doesn’t workwell internationally (i.e. assumes the person you’re communicatingwith knows knows English). If you think the latter is not a problem,imagine how you would feel feel if you had to read a date someonewrote in their native language that you don’t understand:
   The only reason why it does to you is because you’re not used to it.After a little bit of practice, it’ll be even less weird than yourfavorite date format.
  First of all, the claim that the order of relevance is little endianis quite questionable. The only situation when you can say that forcertain is when we’re talking about events occuring on a day-to-daybasis, however I can think of numerous cases when the year and themonth are more relevant:
  Second, the order of relevance is actually irrelevant. Even if theorder of relevance was this way, you’re not reading the dates outloud, so there’s no need for them to be ordered a certain way. Ifyou’re not interested in the year, you just skip it and read the endof the date just like you would do when reading time while not beinginterested in the hour.
  In my opinion, ISO 8601 seems clearly superior to other date formatswhen it comes to international communication (such as posting thingsonline), and as you can see, I have enough reasons to say so. Whilethe format certainly has an audience, it’s unfortunate that it’s notas big as it could be. By writing this article, I hope I made you atleast think about different date formats and be more careful when itcomes to making people understand what date you’re talking about.
title: ISO 8601：更好的日期格式

索尼已经确认，其总部位于东京的内部视频游戏开发商Japan Studio将基本上退出并重新集中于一个团队。 Japan Studio在给IGN的一份声明中说，从4月1日起，它将“重新集中在Astro Playroom背后的创意团队Asobi团队，使该团队可以集中精力实现单一愿景，并进一步发展Astro Playroom的知名度。”换句话说，为更多的Astro游戏做好准备。
 Astro的Playroom是随每个PlayStation 5控制台一起包装的广受欢迎的游戏。这是一款3D平台游戏，旨在展示新型DualSense控制器的高级触觉反馈。在此之前，同一支球队开发了Astro Bot：Rescue Mission，被广泛认为是出色的独家PlayStation VR头衔。
  VGC先前曾报道说，由于索尼拒绝续签其年度合同，日本Studio工作室的“绝大部分”员工将被续签，该合同一直持续到3月31日财政年度结束。据说一些工作人员已被调任至Asobi团队，而据报道其他人也加入了由Silent Hill和Gravity Rush导演富山敬一郎创立的新工作室Bokeh。
 尽管Asobi团队迄今为止的工作广受赞誉，但数十年来，Japan Studio一直是索尼第一方PlayStation开发工作的重要组成部分。它创造了《 Ape Escape》，《 Patapon》和《 LocoRoco》等系列，同时还协助开发了《血源》和《最后的守护者》等游戏。在去年离开之前，富山领导了一个部门，负责“警笛”和“重力奔跑”游戏。
note_en:
Sony has confirmed that Japan Studio, its internal video game developer based in Tokyo, will essentially wind down and refocus around a single team. In a  statement to IGN, Japan Studio said as of April 1st it would be “re-centered to Team Asobi, the creative team behind  Astro’s Playroom, allowing the team to focus on a single vision and build on the popularity of  Astro’s Playroom.” In other words, get ready for more  Astro games.
 Astro’s Playroom is a well-received title packaged with every PlayStation 5 console. It’s a 3D platformer designed to show off the advanced haptic feedback of the new DualSense controller. Before that, the same team developed  Astro Bot: Rescue Mission, widely considered to be the standout exclusive PlayStation VR title.
  VGC previously reported today that the “vast majority” of Japan Studio staff were being let go as Sony declined to renew their annual contracts, which run until the end of the fiscal year on March 31st. Some staff are said to have been reassigned to Team Asobi, while others are reportedly joining Bokeh, a new studio founded by  Silent Hill and  Gravity Rush director Keiichiro Toyama.
 While Team Asobi’s work to date is acclaimed, Japan Studio has been an important part of Sony’s first-party PlayStation development efforts for decades. It created series like  Ape Escape,  Patapon, and  LocoRoco, while also assisting with development on titles like  Bloodborne and  The Last Guardian. Before leaving last year, Toyama led a division that was responsible for the  Siren and  Gravity Rush games.
title: 索尼结束了Japan Studio的工作，专注于Astro的Playroom团队
title_en: Sony winds down Japan Studio to focus on Astro’s Playroom team

随着新闻媒体讨价还价规则大逃杀的尘埃落定，Facebook和澳大利亚政府都宣布了胜利。
  该法案于周二晚上通过了参议院，这意味着政府现在可以让Facebook和Google与新闻生产商进行谈判，以支付出现在搜索引擎或社交媒体上的内容的费用。
 社交媒体巨头在其平台上禁止了澳大利亚新闻之后，Facebook老板马克·扎克伯格（Mark Zuckerberg）与财务主管乔什·弗莱登伯格（Josh Frydenberg）之间在第11小时的谈判中达到高潮，这似乎是一系列威胁和僵局的终结。
 政府获得了议价代码，新闻企业将获得支票，Facebook得到一些关键的让步。
    Facebook几个月来一直反对该提议的代码，并威胁说由于它认为不可行的代码而阻止与新闻文章的链接。
  它认为该代码实际上是可行的-至少进行了一些修改。
 科廷大学互联网研究教授塔玛·利弗说，在扎克伯格和弗莱登伯格之间的一系列电话会议中，社交媒体巨头商讨了一些小而重要的变化，这意味着该代码根本永远不会使用的良机。大学。 
其中一条规定是，在使数字平台成为该代码的前提之前，司库必须首先考虑其是否已与新闻媒体企业达成商业协议。
 该代码将坐在财务主管的办公桌上，并且只要平台足够大以致可以挤钱，他就可以扳动扳机。莱弗教授说。
  莱弗教授说，财务主任尚未表示，Facebook和谷歌必须与新闻生产商达成多少交易以避免该代码，但看来谷歌正在接近。
 谷歌已经与新闻集团，九费尔法克斯，七西传媒，卫报和新闻公司ACM达成了交易。
  ＆＃34;财务主管似乎对与主要参与者达成交易对Google所做的事情感到满意，＆＃34;莱弗教授说。
  如果财务主管曾经决定拉动触发器，那么Facebook可能会再次禁止澳大利亚新闻。
 另一项最新的优惠让公司（或Google）至少有一个月的通知，即该通知将受该代码的约束。 
＆＃34;这意味着Facebook将有一个月的时间进行更多交易来避免它，或者再次发布新闻，这样他们就不会受到它的约束，＆＃34;莱弗教授说。
   莱弗教授说，根据该守则，Facebook将不得不按照严格的规则与新闻制作人进行谈判。
   ＆＃34;在代码之前或代码周围完成交易，意味着Facebook仍在合同规定的范围内呼吁很多人，＆＃34;莱弗教授说。
 我们不知道Facebook将向新闻制作人支付多少费用，但是RMIT大学的媒体法律和政策研究人员詹姆斯·梅斯（James Meese）估计，这可能与它目前向其他国家的新闻制作人支付的使用费用相似他们在Facebook新闻中的内容。
 Facebook News是该应用程序中具有原始新闻功能的选项卡，是该公司在英国推出的一项新功能。
   让它沉入深渊：Facebook最终可能会向新闻制作人支付应以任何方式向他们支付的报酬，而不会受到代码约束的威胁。
  我猜测[新闻发布商]与Facebook的交易将大大少于与Google的交易，＆＃34;莱弗教授说。 
根据该守则，任何收入在15万美元或以上的新闻媒体企业都可以与该平台进行谈判，以支付内容费用。
  根据新的安排，Facebook可能只需要与大型公司达成交易。
 ＆＃34;人们会认为，只要大公司保持快乐，就足够了。这完全取决于司库，梅斯博士说。
  Meese博士说，这使得澳大利亚的区域性市场，通常由较小的媒体提供服务，这是新协议下的最大输家。
   Google与ACM达成了交易，该公司拥有多个大型区域标头，包括《堪培拉时报》，《纽卡斯尔先驱报》，《审查员》，《边境邮报》，《信使》和《伊拉瓦拉水星》。
    他已经在他的大部分网站上收费了。现在，他通过链接到人们无需订阅就无法阅读的内容而获得了收益。
   无论是否使用过议价代码，政府都能得到它想要的东西：大量资金从平台流向新闻出版商。 
Meese博士说，这是迫使技术公司进行谈判的法规威胁。  Facebook终于意识到政府在这里的意图绝不是以编写代码的方式使用该代码。  ＆＃34;目的是将代码用作大锤，迫使平台付款。  播放或暂停的空间，M静音，左箭头和右箭头寻找音量，上箭头和下箭头寻找音量。  上周，当有报道称谷歌已经与澳大利亚媒体公司就价值数千万美元的交易进行了谈判时，财务主管要求信贷。  如果我们没有在议会面前获得立法，这些交易都不会发生。 他当时说。  除了关于金钱的规定外，该代码还包括平台要求，以允许访问参与数据和算法更改通知。 
他们显然已经赢了，但这可能不是有史以来最大的胜利， 梅斯博士说。  如果不能经常使用的话，立法的重点是什么？  梅斯博士说，Facebook，谷歌，澳大利亚政府和新闻企业都从最近的事件中脱颖而出，成为赢家。  Facebook的胜利是以公关灾难为代价的。 无论是偶然还是以其他方式，其新闻禁令都产生了混乱的影响，在西澳大利亚丛林大火中阻止了紧急服务页面上重要信息的访问。  ＆＃34; Facebook认为他们赢了，但我认为他们对房间有误读，＆＃34; 莱弗教授说。  该公司全球事务和沟通副总裁最近发表的一篇博文并未对新闻禁令的破坏表示歉意。  发起了公关活动并威胁要撤回其服务后，它突然回撤并与发行商达成了交易。 
谷歌还可以享受Facebook谈判达成的让步，并最终以其声誉付出代价。  一方面，他们忍受了新闻禁令的破坏，而Facebook将其像政治橄榄球一样对待了大约一周。  另一方面，他们将从现在可能投资于新闻业的数千万美元中受益。  莱弗教授说，但是，这并不能保证新闻公司会将这笔钱用于公益新闻或任何形式的新闻。
note_en:
With the dust settling on the news media bargaining code battle royale, both Facebook and the Australian government have declared victory.
  The legislation  passed the Senate on Tuesday night, meaning the government can now make Facebook and Google negotiate with news producers to pay for content that appears on search engines or social media.
 It's seemingly the end of a series of threats and standoffs that culminated in  11th-hour negotiations between Facebook boss Mark Zuckerberg and Treasurer Josh Frydenberg this week, after the social media giant banned Australian news on its platform.
 The government got its bargaining code, news businesses will get their cheque, and Facebook gets a few key concessions.
    Facebook had been dead against the proposed code for months, threatening to block links to news articles over a code it said  was unworkable.
  It decided the code was, in fact, workable — at least with a few amendments.
 In a series of calls between Mr Zuckerberg and Mr Frydenberg, the social media behemoth negotiated small but important changes that mean there's a good chance the code may never be used at all, said Tama Leaver, a professor of internet studies at Curtin University.
 Among them was the provision that before a digital platform is made subject to the code, the Treasurer must first take into account whether it has reached commercial agreements with news media businesses.
 "The code will sit in the Treasurer's desk and he can pull the trigger whenever a platform is big enough to squeeze for money," Professor Leaver said.
  The Treasurer hasn't said how many deals Facebook and Google must strike with news producers to avoid the code, but it appears that Google is getting close, Professor Leaver said.
 Google has already struck deals with News Corp, Nine Fairfax, Seven West Media, The Guardian and news company ACM.
  "The Treasurer appears happy with what Google has done by striking deals with the major players," Professor Leaver said.
  And if the Treasurer ever decided to "pull the trigger", Facebook could simply ban Australian news again.
 Another last-minute concession gives the company (or Google) at least one month's notice that it will be subject to the code.
 "This means Facebook would have a month to strike more deals to avoid it, or pull news again so they wouldn't be subject to it," Professor Leaver said.
   Under the code, Facebook would have had to negotiate with news producers according to strict rules, Professor Leaver said.
   "Getting deals done before or around the code means Facebook is still calling a lot of the shots in terms of what the contracts specify," Professor Leaver said.
 We don't know how much Facebook will pay news producers, but James Meese, a media law and policy researcher at RMIT University, estimated it could be similar to what it's currently paying news producers in other countries to use their content in Facebook News.
 Facebook News, which is a tab within the app that features original journalism, is a new feature the company is rolling out in the UK.
   Let that sink in: Facebook may end up paying news producers what it would have paid them anyway, without the threat of being subject to the code.
  "I'm guessing the Facebook deal [with news publishers] will be significantly less than the Google deal," Professor Leaver said.
    Under the code, any news media business with revenue of $150,000 or over could negotiate with the platform to be paid for content.
  Under the new arrangement, Facebook probably only needs to strike deals with the big players.
 "One would assume that as long as the big players are kept happy, that may well be enough. It's really up to the Treasurer," Dr Meese said.
  This makes regional Australia, which tends to be served by smaller media outlets, the big loser under the new deal, Dr Meese said.
   Google has struck a deal with ACM, which owns several large regional mastheads including The Canberra Times, The Newcastle Herald, The Examiner, The Border Mail, The Courier and the Illawarra Mercury.
    "He's already paywalled most of his sites. Now he gets money for linking to things that people can't read without a subscription.
   Whether or not the bargaining code is ever used, the government got what it wanted: a large sum of money flowing from platforms to news publishers.
 It's the threat of the code that forced tech companies to negotiate, Dr Meese said.
 "Facebook finally realised that the government's intention here was never to use the code the way it was written.
 "The intention was to use the code as a sledgehammer to force platforms to pay."
 Space to play or pause, M to mute, left and right arrows to seek, up and down arrows for volume.
 Last week, when reports broke that Google had negotiated deals with Australian media companies worth tens of millions of dollars, the Treasurer claimed credit.
 "None of these deals would be happening if we didn't have the legislation before the Parliament," he said at the time.
  Aside from the provisions about money, the code also included requirements for platforms to give access to engagement data and notice of algorithm changes.
 "They've clearly had a win, but it might not be the greatest win of all time," Dr Meese said.
 "What's the point of the bits and bobs of legislation if it may not be used that regularly?"
  Facebook, Google, the Australian government and news businesses all emerge from the recent events as winners, Dr Meese said.
   Facebook's victory came at the cost of a public relations disaster. Either accidentally or otherwise, its news ban had a chaotic effect, blocking access to important information on emergency services pages in the midst of the West Australian bushfires.
 "Facebook thinks they won, but I think they're misreading the room a bit," Professor Leaver said.
  A recent blog post by the company's vice-president of global affairs and communication  did not apologise for the news ban disruption.
  Having mounted a PR campaign and threatening to withdraw its services, it abruptly backtracked and struck deals with publishers instead.
 Google also gets to enjoy the concessions Facebook negotiated and ultimately paid for with its reputation.
  On one hand, they endured the disruption of the news ban and Facebook treating them like a political football for about a week.
 On the other, they will benefit from the tens of millions of dollars that may now be invested in journalism.
 However, there's no guarantee the news companies will use the money for public interest journalism, or any kind of journalism at all, Professor Leaver said.
title: 脸谱网和澳大利亚政府接触：最大的赢家是鲁珀特·默多克
title_en: Facebook and Australian govt stouch: biggest winner is Rupert Murdoch

哦，加油！不要放心，只能去喝可乐或百事可乐。您现在在日本。现在该尝试一些更具冒险精神的事情了。
 1960年开始在全国范围内出现主要用于碳酸饮料的自动售货机。现在，日本全国大约有247万台自动售货机，大约每50人就有1台。因此，很容易以全天候24/7的时间在该国的任何地方获取冷饮或热饮。
 实际上，虽然可口可乐等全球性企业以及三得利等饮料行业的国家巨头都在自动售货机和其他渠道中占据主导地位，但相对晦涩的未成年人仍设法通过多种独特产品来开拓市场。即使是大个子男孩也已经习惯于向他们的营销商授予创造性许可，以远远超出正常的可乐范围。
  在日本，饮料种类繁多，无论是非酒精饮料还是酒精饮料，都是惊人的。喝酒评论家Ryoko Shimizu可以充分理解这一点（顺便说一句，这真是一项很酷的工作！）。自1980年代以来，她一直在审查世界上无酒精的日本饮料。清水最近发布了她的历来最爱的前50名名单（原始文章仅日文）。
   Supodo（スポド）是日语中虚构的英语术语“运动饮料”的缩写。这类软饮料可以补充由于出汗（特别是在运动中）而从体内流失的水分和矿物质。据推测，在烈日下进行体育运动时，它有助于防止脱水和中暑的可能性。
  好的，现在已经定义了这个术语，让我们进入Shimizu的广泛清单，其中包括大量的supodo以及其他类型的饮料！
 并不是所有她的最爱仍然存在，但是，尽管在某些情况下起步很低，但是自那以后，仍然有令人惊讶的数字成为主流。向下滚动以查看Shimizu如何通过从50倒数到1来对它们进行排名： 
佳丽宝（Kanebo）在1990年代出售的Sports Mugicha：清水写道：“当我第一次听到这种饮料的名字时，我的第一印象是它必须是传统大麦茶和'运动饮料'的混合物。实际上，它包括适量盐与普通大麦茶混合使用，以增强其吸收到体内的能力。罐头上装饰着职业棒球队横滨BayStars的主题。”
   百事可乐公司（Pepsi Co.）于1981年推出了Mountain Dew：这款长期畅销的产品至今仍很受欢迎，并在日本以“新柑橘饮料”的口号在市场上销售。这些年来，该程序包几乎没有变化。
   由可口可乐公司于1993年推出的Tab Clear：清水评论说：``由于它是透明的可乐，它成为一个热门话题，但我记得它的味道很微妙。当时颇受欢迎的新闻播音员Kotaro Tawara出现在广告中。”
   Tsubu-Tsubu橙在1990年代由大和（Yamato）出售：这种橙饮料是根据1980年代果泥饮料的趋势而构思的。 “ Tsubu”在日语中的意思是“珠子”或“掉落”。推出时，Tsubu-Tsubu Orange尚未在全国发行。它仅在名古屋附近生产的地方销售。
   麒麟在1990年推出的Post Water：由荔枝制成的这种supodo或“运动饮料”在一个形状异常的烧瓶中首次亮相时就引起了轰动。
   Cheerio Grape由Cheerio于1965年推出：清水深情地回忆起Cheerio的葡萄饮料在她童年时代附近的糖果店出售。
   由三得利（Suntory）在1993年推出的J Water：这种饮料是J联赛刚成立时作为J联赛或日本职业足球（足球）联赛的官方许可饮料而首次亮相的。这种“运动饮料”没有特别鲜明的味道，但是无论如何，这种味道仅次于包装。也有特定于团队的变体。 
1982年由可口可乐公司推出的Ambasa：Shimizu说道：“起初我对陌生的“ Ambasa”一词感到有些不自在，但我很快就习惯了，因为几乎所有可口可乐自动售货机都使用了它。”这种碳酸饮料看起来像乳白色，味道比可尔必思苏打轻。也有一段时间的瓜子和松子味。
   麒麟于1988年推出的Apple Oolong Soda：这是另一种主要依靠包装来刺激销量的饮料。它以动漫《现代儿童》（Modern Children）中的角色为特色，在推出时引起了很多关注。它仍然相当流行。
   由Cheerio于1982年推出的Sweet Kiss：Sweet Kiss是Mountain Dew（＃49）的一种变体，并具有独特的图形设计。但是，即使在东京也很难找到这种饮料。
   乐天（Satte）在1994年由乐天（Lotte）推出了萨罗猴王（Saruo Monkey King）：萨罗这个名字的字面意思是日语的``猴子王''。它最初是作为一种独特的乌龙茶销售的，这种乌龙茶是由在猴子喜欢觅食的悬崖上种植的茶叶制成的。当然，对于猴子是否真的收获茶叶存在一些疑问。据报道，其中一名受访者在味觉测试中说：“闻起来像猴子。”幸运的是，对于这个品牌，还有很多其他人不同意！
   麒麟于1986年推出的下午茶：这种饮料是畅销时间最长的一种。它相当甜，是第一个装在1.5升PET瓶中的红茶。
   Bireley的Orange由旭（Asahi）于1951年推出：这种超长跑运动员的名字“ Birely’s”曾经是橙汁的代名词。自1970年代以来，当实行限制措施来限制营销人员将饮料标注为果汁（除非它是100％果汁）的能力时，Birely不得不放弃对果汁的引用。但是，这次小小的挫折并没有对其市场份额造成重大影响。该品牌今天仍在销售。
   Soka Komatsuen于1997年推出了北陆苏打哥斯拉松井罐头：这是一种本地生产的饮料，旨在帮助使著名的棒球选手松井秀树（Hideki Matsui）永生化，他也是石川县的``最爱儿子''。松井，绰号“哥斯拉”，是本垒打王，后来为纽约洋基队效力。 
三得利于1986年推出的蜂蜜柠檬：这种水果饮料一经发布便经历了爆炸性的销售。三得利有一个问题。由于他们无法在“日本柠檬”中注册“蜂蜜柠檬”甚至是其昵称的商标，因此该特定名称实际上成为了其自己的类别，因为其他几家制造商也向该市场投放了相同名称的类似饮料。这是一个典型的例子，说明了日本无酒精饮料行业的现状。
   MMC在1982年由MMC推出：这个收藏家的物品早已从市场上撤下。显然，即使在1990年代中期就可以找到它，也相对难以找到。 McCOL被以“大麦可乐”的名义销售，但它看起来与其他可乐一样。味道是大麦茶和苹果酒的结合。
   TESS奶茶在1980年代末由三得利（Suntory）出售：TESS作为半甜型奶茶的使用寿命相对较短。
   由三得利（Suntory）于1981年推出的NCAA：以美国国家大学体育协会（NCAA）的名字命名，这款经典运动饮料被称为“对热爱运动的人的全新止渴剂”。
   由三得利（Suntory）在1990年推出的Atamaruko：这种金橘和calamansi（又名“菲律宾石灰”）的柑橘混合物实际上是在自动售货机的加热部分中热卖的。实际上，它的名字意思是“一个让您热身的孩子”。可爱的包装设计被证明可以有效地吸引顾客。实际上，这导致了第二代角色“ Nacchan”的出现，甚至比Atamaruko更加出名，后者被贬为“不知名的姐姐”。
   佳得乐（Yator Jijirushi）在1980年代出售了佳得乐（Gatorade）：美国超级品牌佳得乐（Gatorade）已由本土竞争对手波加利汗（Pocari Sweat）取代，不再在日本的罐头中出售。不过，这是日本首次出现的“运动饮料”。那时，佳得乐仅以粉末形式提供。
   中松博士在1994年由Cheerio推出的Head Tea：现在，我们进入了真正奇怪的领域。这款强力的混合茶以刚推出时的“智能食品”的领先研究者中松阳四郎博士的名字命名。多年后，同一位中松博士（Nakamatsu）在2005年获得了Ig贵族奖（Ig Noble Prize），从而在日本以外获得了更多的声名狼藉。这项讽刺奖被授予了十项科学研究的小成就。它的既定目标是“荣誉成就，这些成就首先使人们发笑，然后使他们思考。”嗯... 
三得利于1993年推出的Pekoe直茶：Pekoe是TESS的后继产品（请参阅第34页），以可爱的设计推向市场，也被改编为流行的电视广告。在其中，一个女学生跳舞时以鹅妈妈的故事为歌词的歌曲。目的是让年轻女性与一罐Pekoe一起合影。
  尽管三得利在2001年将该品牌卖给了竞争对手立顿茶，但这种设计的元素在包装上仍然很明显。
   Takara在1990年推出的香蕉可乐：清水坦率地承认被包装吸引了。不过，她只买了一个罐头，因为它的味道和听起来一样差。毕竟，那是香蕉味的可乐。
   朝日公司（Asahi）于1972年推出了三谷苹果酒（Mitsuya Cider）：这种超长跑者的历史可以追溯到19世纪的明治时代。这种特殊的瓶子设计可以追溯到1972年，并且一直沿用至今。它有点像可重复使用的啤酒瓶。
   由Pokka于1991年推出的Pudding Shake：现在，我们进入了“怪异和精彩的。”直到今天，这种“饮料”仍然可以罐装出售。它实际上是由布丁组成的，必须先将其摇晃并与糖浆混合，然后才能饮用。 2012年，Pokka与Sapporo合并，从而大大减少了Pokka SKU。布丁奶昔（Budding Shake）仍然有大量忠实支持者，他们可以帮助维持该品牌的生命力。
   大原（Ohara）在1960年推出的Co-up Guarans：这种稀有的碳酸饮料是由中小型区域性饮料制造商的合作社以统一商标制成的，旨在与大型公司生产的可乐竞争。图中的瓶子是由北海道函馆市的一家饮料制造商生产的。它是由“ Ohara”生产的，装在500毫升的瓶子中。函馆市的中学生和高中生似乎在俱乐部活动回家的路上曾经一次喝过这个大瓶子。虽然Ohara不再销售这种特殊的可重复使用的瓶子，但他们仍然出售一次性瓶子和PET瓶子的变体。
   大同（Daido）在1996年推出的Mistio Grapefruit：大同（Daido）首次推出Mistio时实际上领先于当时的时代，Mistio是一种仅含少量碳酸的饮料。如今，许多竞争对手的碳酸化程度很小。它有葡萄和橙两种版本。 
但是，真正有用的是一个受欢迎的电视广告，其中以流行歌手偶像安室奈美惠（Name Amuro）为例，他简单地宣称：“我认为它足够美味，可以再次订购。”现在好了！仅此而已？
   Pokka咖啡由Pokka于1972年推出：虽然有很多类似的东西，但比赛没有罐子周围包裹的独特手绘面孔！这张脸之前是一个聚会现场。可以这么说，它在1999年进行了“翻新”（请参见右侧的设计）。这款著名的杯子于2019年重新推出，并进行了逐步改进，现已发展到第13代。
   UCC咖啡于1969年由UCC推出：这是罐装咖啡领域的开创性最初参与者。它发行后的一年，在1970年的大阪世博会上售出后，出现了爆炸性的销售记录。 UCC Coffee仍然是该行业的主导者。首次发布时，罐子的设计实际上包含两个小孔，一个用于饮用，另一个用于排出空气以方便倒出。
   百事可乐公司（Pepsi Co.）在1970年代出售的Mirinda柠檬青柠：碳酸饮料的一种便宜些的芬达仿制品，这种碳酸饮料在日本不再销售。但是，仍然可以在国外或在线找到它。百事可乐公司Mirinda的商标现在归三得利所有。
   三得利于1995年推出的全富：这种碳酸饮料含有中草药提取物。它在男歌手SMAP乐队的主要成员演员演员Shingo Katori的首演中广受欢迎。
   2本（1995年）香取慎吾pic.twitter.com/dHLy6QcklG
-minipops（@ minipops5）2020年8月19日 
Jolt Cola于1990年推出，随后于2001年由UCC退休：对顾客规定Jolt Cola十年的长期运营，因为他们注意到饮用可乐的量是普通可乐的两倍后，他们的联系甚至更多。事实证明，许多人的期望与现实不符。
   麒麟于1987年推出的You Watermelon Soda，虽然不再具有像西瓜一样的独特设计，但在2010年用PET瓶重新推出了第三代版本。西瓜。
   可口可乐公司于1970年代出售的芬达金葡萄（Fanta Golden Grape）：这个词是因为该产品的生产问题而诞生的，该染料使芬达葡萄呈现紫色。由于可以使用产生较浅（金色）颜色的技术，因此富有创意的营销人员必须在最后一刻疯狂地更改产品名称。但是，在推出Fanta Golden Apple之后，这种味道就被淘汰了。
   武田在1958年推出的+ C：+ C本质上是橙汁，其添加的果肉中含有额外的纤维含量。它是由日本最大的制药商武田制药的子公司开发和销售的。
   Kagome于1984年推出了Rivella：这种碳酸乳状饮料由Kagome从瑞士进口。
   日本烟草（JT）于1996年推出的天然桃子水：在日本以其昵称“ momoten”而闻名的JT在首次投入巨资投资电视广告时就引起了轰动。 JT于2015年退出饮料业务时，该饮料及其Roots Coffee品牌都卖给了三得利。
   Kagome于1995年推出了Rhinoceros Cider：用日语单词播放，这种饮料的名字叫``Sai-no-Cider''。谐音“ sai”（日语中的犀牛）听起来像是苹果酒的第一个音节。得到它？除了包装，这种苹果酒真的没有什么特别的。 
B＆amp;卡尔皮斯（Calpis）在1980年代出售的L Bitter and Lemon（柠檬味和柠檬味）：这种碳酸饮料可能已经超前了，并且上市时间不长。它具有苦柠檬的味道。
   百事可乐公司（Pepsi Co.）于1991年推出的Nippon Cha-Cha-Cha：清水对此饮料相当批评，称其为``非常普通的绿茶''。但是，民族包装是百事可乐公司的一个有趣选择。
    百佳（Pokka）于1973年推出的柠檬茶：根据制造商的说法，百佳（Pokka）的柠檬茶是世界上第一个罐装红茶。这种特殊的茶加了很多糖，使糖变甜。
   1960年代由Shinkoya售出的Corona Pine Cider苹果酒：Corona Pine Cider苹果酒是由山形县一家日本传统糖果店生产的菠萝味苹果酒，如今已是一个臭名昭著的名称。它的初衷是为初中和高中学生放学回家时提供的清凉饮料，他们的野心相对有限。尽管如此，Corona Pine Cider后来在其不起眼的起点之后仍获得了忠实拥护。但是，如今几乎不可能找到这种饮料。
   百事可乐公司（Pepsi Co）在1990年推出了Jazz'in：标语“人人都可以喝的成人饮料”被用于营销这种“茶水”。这个想法是要平衡茶叶的苦味和苏打水的甜味。
   TERRA由Aji-no-Moto于1983年推出：这种“运动饮料”被定位为富含氨基酸的等渗饮料。除了普通的蓝色外，它还具有苹果和柠檬之类的味道。罐子也设计有不同的颜色。
   娜玛·穆吉（Nama Mugi）娜玛·国美（Nama国美）娜玛·塔玛（Nama Tamago）在1980年代仅被麒麟（Kirin）短暂出售，仅以“一个季节”售出。这种饮料以一种流行的日本绕口令命名，并带有传统日本讲故事者或菊苣的插图，实际上是一种奶昔。 
由三得利（Suntory）于1991年推出的热血饮料：这种运动饮料含有辛二醇，一种通常从甘蔗中蒸馏出的天然成分，据说有助于燃烧脂肪。
  显然，它的挑衅性名字是围绕它的首个电视广告而建立的。在其中，主要演员变成了一个“热血小子”，他从一个“邪恶组织”中救出了一个女主人公，同时还吞下了热饮。
   Smap！麒麟（Kirin）在2002年推出的产品：这种棕色碳酸饮料在口味上一无所获。一切都与包装有关。 Smap当时以＃1男孩乐队的名字命名！这只是营销组合的一部分，目的是向年轻女性出售更多CD。
   由Bokka在1994年推出的Bobocha：Bobocha是中国乌龙茶茶叶，中国普’茶茶叶和沙丁鱼的混合物。制片由成龙（Jackie Chan）监督，当时成龙在日本非常流行。
  他出现在电视广告中的这种受欢迎的茶中，并因其误称日语“我在喝酒”而深深地被人们铭记。
   Sasuke由三得利（Suntory）于1984年推出：清水解释说，在饮料世界中，相当于音乐的“一击奇迹”的这种东西在大胆地将自己定位为“敢于在可乐面前穿越的家伙”之后，很快就被撤出了市场。 。” …在这里，我认为这与热门电视节目有关，该电视节目针对业余运动员进行了耐力测试！
    Wekka由Pokka于1987年推出：最初是为中国奥运会运动员在中国开发的，这款传奇的运动饮料是清水的最爱。它似乎使几代日本人怀念世纪之交前的独特罐头饮料的鼎盛时期。 
那么，您认识其中几个？ 尽管有些公司仍在发展壮大，但其他公司早已被淘汰，只存在于日本软饮料行业历史的史册中。  无论如何，到现在为止，您必须口渴！ 是时候来一些Bobocha（＃3），Sweet Kiss（＃41）或也许是McCOL（＃35）了。 我想我会去搜寻Mercari来抢一罐Nakamatsu博士的头茶（＃30）！  7.3级地震的发生地点与十年前东日本大地震的震中大致相同。
note_en:
Oh come on! Don’t play it safe and only go just go for a Coke or Pepsi. You’re in Japan now. It’s time to try something more adventurous.
 Vending machines mainly for carbonated drinks started appearing across the country in 1960.  Now there are an estimated 2,470,000 of them throughout Japan or 1 for approximately every 50 people.  Thus, it is easy to grab either a cold or hot drink virtually anywhere in the country on a 24/7 basis.
 While the same global players such as Coca-Cola and national powerhouses in the beverage industry like Suntory do, in fact, dominate distribution via vending machines and other channels, relatively obscure minor entrants have still managed to carve out a niche with several unique offerings.  Even the big boys have become used to granting a creative license to their marketers to go well beyond normal colas.
  The sheer variety of beverages— both non-alcoholic and alcoholic—available in Japan is staggering.  To make sense of it all is soft drink critic Ryoko Shimizu (aside– now that’s a cool job!).  She has been reviewing the world of non-alcoholic Japanese beverages since the 1980s.  Shimizu recently published a  Top 50 list of her all-time favorites (the original article in Japanese only).
   Supodo (スポド) is an abbreviation for the made-up English term “sports drink” in Japanese.  This category is a type of soft drink that replenishes the water and minerals lost from the body due to perspiration, especially during exercise.  It supposedly helps to prevent dehydration and the potential for heatstroke while playing sports under the scorching sun.
  Okay, now that this term has been defined, let’s get to Shimizu’s extensive list which includes plenty of  supodo as well as other types of drinks!
 Not all of her favorites are still around, but, despite humble beginnings in some cases, a surprising number have since gone mainstream.  Scroll down to see how Shimizu ranked them by counting backward from 50 down to 1:
  Sports Mugicha sold in the 1990s by Kanebo:  Shimizu wrote, “When I first heard the name of this drink, my first impression was that it must be a blend of traditional barley tea and a ‘sports drink.’  It consists, in fact, of a combination of an appropriate amount of salt mixed with regular barley tea to enhance its absorption into the body.  The can was decorated with a theme from the professional baseball team Yokohama BayStars.”
   Mountain Dew launched in 1981 by Pepsi Co:  This long-seller, which is still popular today, is marketed in Japan with the tag line “new citrus beverage.”  The package has changed little over the years.
   Tab Clear launched in 1993 by Coca-Cola:  Shimizu commented, “It became a hot topic because it was a transparent cola, but I remember that it was subtle in terms of taste. Kotaro Tawara, a popular newscaster at the time, appeared in a commercial.”
   Tsubu-Tsubu Orange was sold in the 1990s by Yamato:  This orange drink was conceived based upon the trend of mashed fruit drinks in the 1980s.  “Tsubu” means “bead” or “drop” in Japanese.  When it was launched, Tsubu-Tsubu Orange lacked national distribution.  It was only marketed locally in Nagoya, near where it was made.
   Post Water launched in 1990 by Kirin:  Made from lychees, this  supodo or “sports drink” made quite a scene when it debuted in an unusually-shaped flask.
   Cheerio Grape launched in 1965 by Cheerio:  Shimizu fondly recalls Cheerio’s Grape drink as being for sale at the candy store near her childhood home.
   J Water launched in 1993 by Suntory:  This drink made its debut as an officially licensed beverage of the J League or Japan Professional Football (soccer) League back when J League was new.  This “sports drink” did not have a particularly distinctive taste, but the taste was secondary to packaging in any case.  There are also team-specific variants.
   Ambasa launched in 1982 by Coca-Cola:  Shimizu commented, “I felt a little  uncomfortable with the unfamiliar word ‘Ambasa’ at first, but I quickly got used to it because it was put into virtually every Coca-Cola vending machine.”  This carbonated beverage looks milky and tastes lighter than Calpis Soda.  There were also melon and pine flavors for a while.
   Apple Oolong Soda launched in 1988 by Kirin:  This is another drink that relies primarily upon packaging to drum up sales.  It features characters from the anime Modern Children (いまどきのこども), which attracted a lot of attention when launched. It is still fairly popular.
   Sweet Kiss launched in 1982 by Cheerio:  Sweet Kiss is sort of a variant of Mountain Dew (#49) and features a distinctive graphic design.  It is, however, still relatively difficult to find this drink even in Tokyo.
   Saruo Monkey King launched in 1994 by Lotte:  The name Saruo literally means “monkey king” in Japanese.  It was originally marketed as an exclusive type of oolong tea made from tea leaves grown on cliffs where monkeys like to forage food.  There is, naturally, some question about whether monkeys actually harvest tea leaves.  One of the respondents reportedly remarked in a taste test, “It sort of smells like a monkey.”  Luckily for the brand, there are, however, plenty of other people who disagree!
   Afternoon Tea launched in sold in 1986 by Kirin:  This drink is a best selling long-runner.  It is fairly sweet and was the first black tea sold in a 1.5 liter PET bottle.
   Bireley’s Orange launched in 1951 by Asahi:  The name of this ultra-long-runner, “Birely’s,” used to be synonymous orange juice. Since the 1970s, when restrictions were introduced that limited marketers’ ability to label a drink as juice unless it was 100% juice, Birely’s had to drop the reference to juice.  This minor setback did, however, not make a major dent in its market share. The brand is still sold today.
   Hokuriku Soda Godzilla Matsui Can launched in 1997 by Soka Komatsuen:  This is a locally produced drink made to help immortalize the famous baseball player Hideki Matsui who was also a “favorite son” of Ishikawa Prefecture.  Matsui, whose nickname was “Godzilla,” was a home run king who went on to play for the New York Yankees.
   Honey Lemon launched in 1986 by Suntory:  This fruit drink experienced explosive sales upon its release.  Suntory had a problem, though.  As they could not register a trademark for “honey lemon” or even its nickname in Japanese  hachi-lemo, this particular name essentially became its own category because several other manufacturers flooded the market with similar drinks by the same name.  It is a classic example of just how cut-throat the non-alcoholic drink industry can be in Japan.
   McCOL launched in 1982 by MMC:  This collector’s item has long since been taken off the market.  It was, apparently, relatively hard to find even when it was available through the mid-1990s.  McCOL was marketed as “barley cola,” but it looked just like any other cola.  The taste was a combination of barley tea and cider.
   TESS Milk Tea sold in the late 1980s by Suntory:  TESS had a relatively short lifetime as a semi-sweet type of milk tea.
   NCAA launched in 1981 by Suntory:  Named for the National College Athletic Association (NCAA), this classic sports drink was introduced as “the brand new thirst quencher for those who love sports.”
   Atamaruko launched in 1990 by Suntory:  This citrus mixture of kumquats and calamansi (aka “Philippine lime”) was actually sold hot in the heated section of vending machines. Its name means, in fact, “A Child to Warm You Up.”  The cute package design proved effective at attracting customers.  It actually led to a second-generation character called “Nacchan” that became even more famous than Atamaruko, who was relegated to the status of “the unknown older sister.”
   Gatorade sold in the 1980s by Yukijirushi:  Having been replaced by homegrown rival Pocari Sweat, American uber-brand Gatorade is no longer sold in cans in Japan. It was, though, the first “sports drink” to appear in Japan.  At that point, Gatorade was only available as a powder.
   Dr. Nakamatsu’s Head Tea launched in 1994 by Cheerio:  Now we’re entering the realm of the truly odd.  Named for Dr. Yoshiro Nakamatsu, a leading researcher of “smart foods” at the time of launch, this strong blended tea was somehow supposed to enhance cognitive capability.  Years later, the same Dr. Nakamatsu gained additional notoriety outside Japan by winning the “Ig Noble Prize” in 2005.  This satiric prize is awarded to ten trivial achievements in scientific research.  Its stated aim is to “honor achievements that first make people laugh, and then make them think.” Hmmm…
   Pekoe Straight Tea launched in 1993 by Suntory:  The successor to TESS (see #34), Pekoe was marketed with a cute design, which was also adapted into a popular television commercial.  In it, a schoolgirl girl dances to a song with the story of Mother Goose as the lyrics.  It was intended to get young women to take pictures posing with a can of Pekoe.
  Elements of this design are still evident in the packaging, even though Suntory sold this brand to rival Lipton Tea in 2001.
   Banana Cola launched in 1990 by Takara:  Shimizu candidly admitted being attracted by the packaging.  She only purchased a single can once, though, as it ended up tasting just as bad as it sounds. This was, after all, a banana-scented cola.
   Mitsuya Cider launched in 1972 by Asahi:  The history of this ultra-long-runner dates back to the Meiji Era in the 19th century.  This particular bottle design dates from 1972 and is still in use today.  It is sort of like a reusable beer bottle.
   Pudding Shake launched in 1991 by Pokka:  Now we’re getting into the “Weird & Wonderful.”  This “drink” is still sold in a can to this day.  It is actually comprised of pudding, which needs to be shaken and mixed with liquid syrup before you can drink it. Pokka merged with Sapporo back in 2012, which led to a significant reduction in Pokka SKUs. The Pudding Shake still has a sizeable base of diehard fans who help to keep the brand alive.
   Co-up Guarans launched in 1960 by Ohara:  This rare carbonated drink is made under a unified trademark by a cooperative of small to medium-sized regional beverage manufacturers to compete with colas manufactured by major companies. The pictured bottle was made by a beverage manufacturer in Hakodate, Hokkaido.  It was produced by “Ohara” and came in a 500ml bottle. It seems that middle and high school students in Hakodate used to drink this large bottle all at once on their way home from club activities.  While Ohara no longer markets this particular re-usable bottle, they still sell a single-use bottle and PET bottle variants.
   Mistio Grapefruit launched in 1996 by Daido:  Daido was actually ahead of its time when it first came out with Mistio, a drink that was only slightly carbonated.  Nowadays, there are lots of competitors with just a small degree of carbonation.  It came in both grape and orange versions.
  However, what really helped was a popular television commercial that featured the pop singer idol Namie Amuro who simply claimed, “I consider it delicious enough to want to order it again.”  Well now!  That’s all it took?
   Pokka Coffee launched in 1972 by Pokka:  While there are many like it, the competition just does not have that distinctive, hand-drawn face wrapped around the can!  The face was preceded by a party scene.  It got a “facelift,” so to speak, in 1999 (see the design on the right).  Re-launched in 2019 with incremental improvements, that famous mug is now in its 13 generations.
   UCC Coffee launched in 1969 by UCC:  This was the ground-breaking initial entrant in the canned coffee segment.  It recorded explosive sales after being sold at the Osaka Expo in 1970, a year after its release.  UCC Coffee is still the dominant player in the industry.  When it was first released, the design of the can actually incorporated two small holes, one for drinking and another for letting air out to facilitate pouring.
   Mirinda Lemon Lime sold in the 1970s by Pepsi Co:  A slightly cheaper knock-off of Fanta from Coca-Cola, this carbonated beverage is no longer sold in Japan.  It can, however, still be found outside the country and on-line.  Pepsi Co’s trademark for Mirinda is now owned by Suntory.
   Quan Foo launched in 1995 by Suntory:  This carbonated drink contains Chinese herbal extracts.  It was made popular by the actor Shingo Katori, the main member of the boy band SMAP, at the launch.
   サントリー カンフーCM。2本（1995）香取慎吾  pic.twitter.com/dHLy6QcklG
— minipops (@minipops5)  August 19, 2020
  Jolt Cola launched in 1990 and subsequently retired in 2001 by UCC:  Jolt Cola’s decade long run was stipulated on customers noticing that they would be even more wired after drinking cola with twice the amount of caffeine as regular colas.  It turned out that expectations did not match reality for many.
   With You Watermelon Soda launched in 1987 by Kirin:  While no longer available in its distinctive design that looks like a watermelon, a third-generation version was relaunched in a PET bottle in 2010.  To this day, there are few competitors with the taste of watermelon.
   Fanta Golden Grape sold in the 1970s by Coca-Cola:  The word is that this product was born from a production problem with the dye used to make Fanta Grape appear purple.  As the technology to produce a lighter (golden) color was available, creative marketers must have frantically changed the name of the product at the last minute.  This flavor was, however, phased out after Fanta Golden Apple was launched.
   + C launched in 1958 by Takeda:  + C is, essentially, orange juice, which has extra fiber content from the added pulp.  It was developed and marketed by a subsidiary of Takeda Pharmaceutical, Japan’s largest drugmaker.
   Rivella launched in 1984 by Kagome:  This carbonated milky drink is imported from Switzerland by Kagome.
   Natural Peach Water launched in 1996 by Japan Tobacco (JT):  Known in Japan by its nickname “ momoten,” JT made quite a splash when it first launched by investing heavily in television advertising.  Both this drink and its Roots Coffee brand were sold to Suntory when JT withdrew from the beverage business in 2015.
   Rhinoceros Cider launched in 1995 by Kagome:  A play on Japanese words, the name of this drink is called “Sai-no-Cider.” The homonym “sai,” which means rhinoceros in Japanese, sounds like the English term cider’s first syllable.  Get it? Other than the packaging, there is really nothing special about this type of cider.
   B & L Bitter and Lemon sold in the 1980s by Calpis:  This carbonated drink may have been ahead of its time and was not around for very long.  It featured the taste of bitter lemon.
   Nippon Cha-Cha-Cha launched in 1991 by Pepsi Co:  Shimizu was quite critical of this drink, calling it just a “very ordinary green tea.”  The nationalistic packaging was, however, an interesting choice from Pepsi Co.
    Lemon Tea launched in 1973 by Pokka:  According to the manufacturer, Pokka’s Lemon Tea was the world’s first canned black tea.  This particular tea was sweetened with lots of sugar, which proved to be too much for some.
   Corona Pine Cider sold in the 1960s by Shinkoya:  A regional specialty with a now-infamous name, Corona Pine Cider was a pineapple-flavored cider produced by a traditional Japanese candy shop in Yamagata Prefecture.  It started with relatively limited ambitions as a refreshing drink for junior and senior high school students on their way home from school. Still, Corona Pine Cider subsequently achieved a loyal following beyond its humble beginnings.  Nowadays, it is, though, virtually impossible to find this drink.
   Jazz’in launched in 1990 by Pepsi Co:  The tag line “Everybody’s good adult drink” was used for marketing this “tea soda.”  The idea was to balance the bitterness of tea leaves and the sweetness of soda water.
   TERRA launched in 1983 by Aji-no-Moto:  This “sports drink” was positioned as an amino acid-rich isotonic beverage.  In addition to the normal blue, it had flavors such as apple and lemon.  The cans were also designed in different colors.
   Nama Mugi Nama Gome Nama Tamago sold briefly for only “one season” in the 1980s by Kirin:  This drink, which was named for a popular Japanese tongue twister and featured an illustration of a traditional Japanese storyteller or  rakugoka, was actually a milkshake.
   Hot Blooded Drink launched in 1991 by Suntory:  This sports drink contained octacosanol, a natural ingredient often distilled from sugar cane that is said to help burn fat.
  Its provocative name was, apparently, built around its debut television commercial.  In it, the main actor is transformed into a “hot-blooded kid” who rescues a heroine from an “evil organization” all while gulping down a Hot-Blooded Drink.
   Smap! launched in 2002 by Kirin:  Nothing about this brown carbonated drink stood out in terms of taste.  It was all about the packaging.  Named for the #1 boy band at the time, Smap! was simply part of the marketing mix to sell more CDs, mostly to young women.
   Bobocha launched in 1994 by Pokka:  Bobocha is a blend of Chinese oolong tea leaves, Chinese Pu’er tea leaves, and sardines.  Production was supervised by Jackie Chan, who was at the height of his popularity in Japan at the time.
  He appeared in the television commercials for this popular tea and is fondly remembered for subtly mispronouncing the Japanese term for “I’m drinking.”
   Sasuke launched in 1984 by Suntory:  Shimizu explained that this equivalent in the world of beverages to music’s “one-hit wonder” ended up being taken off the market relatively soon after boldly positioning itself “for the guy who dares to cross in front of cola.”  …and here I thought it had something to do with the hit TV show featuring a fitness endurance test for amateur athletes!
    Weili launched in 1987 by Pokka:  Originally developed in China for Chinese Olympic athletes, this legendary sports drink is Shimizu’s favorite.  It seems to make generations of Japanese nostalgic for the heyday of unique canned drinks just before the turn of the century.
   So, how many of these do you recognize?  While some are still going strong, others have long since gone by the wayside and only live on in the annals of the history of the Japanese soft drink industry.
 In any case, by now you have got to be thirsty!  It’s time for some Bobocha (#3), a Sweet Kiss (#41), or, perhaps, a McCOL (#35).  I think that I’ll go scout out Mercari to snag a can of Dr. Nakamatsu’s Head Tea (#30)!
 Magnitude 7.3 earthquake strikes in roughly the same location as the epicenter of the Great East Japan Earthquake a decade ago.
title: 日本销售的最疯狂的饮料
title_en: Top Craziest Drinks Sold in Japan

note:
今天，许多男人的精子数量只有祖父的一半。令人震惊的发现于2017年由纽约西奈山伊坎医学院环境与生殖流行病学家Shanna Swan博士发表。
 在对涉及近45,000名健康男性的185项研究进行分析之后，Swan和她的团队发现，在过去的40年中，西方国家男性的精子数量下降了50％以上。
 为什么大幅下降？斯旺说，有许多因素在起作用-酗酒，吸烟，体重和缺乏运动。但是她已经着手研究另一个更阴险的原因：接触会干扰人体激素生成的常见化学物质。
 过去30年来，Swan一直在研究这些所谓的内分泌干扰物。事实证明，男人的精子并不是唯一会影响他们的东西：它们也可能以更广泛的方式改变人类的性发育和生殖。在女孩中，接触此类化学物质与青春期的较早发作有关。同时，妇女的鸡蛋质量下降，流产增加。
 在新书《倒计时：我们的现代世界如何威胁精子数量，改变男性和女性生殖发育以及危害人类未来》中，天鹅概述了这些化学物质对我们的生育力造成的隐性损害以及我们为什么必须采取行动现在，以防止它们造成进一步的损害。
 在书中，她提出了一个挑衅性的主张：人类可能没有更长久自然繁殖的能力。她估计，到2050年，全球很大一部分人口将需要辅助生殖技术来繁殖。我与Swan进行了交谈，以了解有关这些化学暴露的更多信息，以及可以采取哪些措施来避免生育不育的未来。
   珊娜·斯旺（Shanna Swan）：我们谈论的是数百种（即使不是数千种）-因为大多数未经测试-我们日常生活中的化学物质。我研究最多的内分泌干扰物是邻苯二甲酸盐。这是一大类化学物质。它们中的几种使塑料柔软而有弹性。其他具有促进吸收和保留的能力。它们被放入个人护理产品中。例如，它们帮助洗手液穿过皮肤，帮助气味进入您的鼻子。它们还帮助农药进入植物。它们被添加到许多产品中。 
食物最常暴露于邻苯二甲酸盐。这有点令人惊讶，但这是因为所有处理食品和包装食品的软管道和软覆盖物中都含有邻苯二甲酸盐。在邻苯二甲酸盐的存在下，热量与食物的结合是不好的结合。它们对人类有很多影响，而我最担心的是它们降低睾丸激素的能力。
 然后是双酚，它们是作用于雌激素的化合物，而邻苯二甲酸盐是对睾丸激素起作用的化合物。它们使塑料变硬。它们可以在婴儿奶瓶，饮用水瓶和其他产品中找到。最熟悉的是BPA，但也有BPS，BPF等替代品。
 另一类非常令人关注的化学物质是杀虫剂，它们一直追溯到DDT，目前一直到Roundup。根据特定的配方，这些激素在许多方面具有荷尔蒙活性。他们进入我们的食物。在职业上处理这些产品的人中也发现了它们。农药是暴露的主要来源，也是我们内分泌系统的真正风险。
 然后是阻燃剂，这是一类化学物质，多年来受到了广泛的关注。他们已经改变，他们变得“明显”更安全，但从未真正安全。这些化学物质存在于从孩子的睡衣到消防泡沫再到我们沙发上的垫子等各种物品中。他们真的很难摆脱我们的家。
 然后是一类称为PFAS的化学物质。这些是化学物质，例如在特氟龙中用于使物品具有防粘性。它们也使东西防水，例如我们的雨衣。这些化学物质具有很强的荷尔蒙作用，特别是对甲状腺和免疫系统。
 您提到了这些化学物质对男性生育的影响，但是女性的生育呢？
 很难学习。我对特定激素激活剂的大部分工作都针对男性。但是越来越多的研究表明，它们也可以改变女性体内激素的水平。它们会干扰月经功能，月经初潮的年龄，更年期的年龄，卵巢早衰等。 
对男人的研究要好得多，因为传统上，对男人的研究总是比较好。直到最近，美国国立卫生研究院甚至都没有要求您学习两性。其次，在男性中，很容易看到器官和精子。检查女人的卵子和卵巢比较困难。您必须进行超声波检查。因此，由于许多原因，女性很难学习。
 生活方式因素，如饮食和运动，以及社会因素，如等待时间更长的孩子，也与生育能力下降有关。我们能将这些化学药品归咎于多少？
 这是一个很好的问题，我不知道答案。根据我的工作，我认为这是实质性的。但是，即使看起来与这些化学物质有很大不同的生活方式因素也没有太大的不同。例如，影响生育力的一种生活方式因素是肥胖。这些化学物质中的许多是致肥胖物。它们导致肥胖，干扰与饱腹感和新陈代谢有关的激素。它们使我们更容易发胖，因此肥胖处于暴露与不孕之间。
 让我们再举一个例子。为了生产婴儿，您显然必须具有强大的性功能。现在，我们有大量证据表明这些破坏内分泌的化学物质会干扰性功能。在中国进行的一项研究中，制造双酚A的男性有更多的性问题和勃起功能障碍。在我们的研究中，我们询问女性对性生活的满意度如何，而邻苯二甲酸盐含量较高的女性则对性生活的满意度较低。因此，您可以看到婴儿的制造步骤很多，并且化学品在整个过程中都有很多机会干扰这些婴儿。这可能不是直接的影响，这就是很难量化的原因之一。
 我们知道，随着年龄的增长，女性卵子的质量会下降，从而影响生育能力。邻苯二甲酸盐等化学物质会加剧这种情况吗？
 女人的卵的质量会受到环境化学物质的影响。哈佛有一个计划，一个名为“环境与生殖健康（EARTH）研究”的研究计划。在那项研究中，他们所做的是，他们将夫妇进行IVF等辅助生殖，并从男人和女人那里获取尿液和血液样本。他们在多篇论文中表明，当母亲和父亲暴露于多种环境化学物质中时，卵和胚胎的质量较差，成功分娩的人较少。这说明卵子和精子的质量。它们都很重要。它们都必须是高质量的，并且都可能因环境化学物质而受损。
 我听到的很多解释是，人们选择生育的孩子少了，而生育率下降是因为他们选择推迟生育，这令人非常沮丧。如果不是那样的话，生育就好了。嗯，这实际上是不正确的，因为我们看到的许多生育问题都存在于年轻女性中。实际上，我们使用《全国家庭增长调查》数据撰写了一篇有关此事的论文。我们表明，年轻妇女的问题实际上比老年妇女的问题高。因此，我不认为生育力下降仅是由于推迟生育而造成的。 
避免破坏内分泌的化学物质是否像不购买BPA塑料或不食用不含农药的有机食品那样简单？
 尽管这些方法有所帮助，但并不是那么简单。有研究表明，儿童和成人食用有机食品，并显示出许多积极的方面。在一项针对年轻人的研究中，我们发现当男人吃有机食品时，他们的精液质量会更好。因此，我认为这肯定会有所帮助，但困难的是，我们的大部分曝光都是隐藏的。您无法始终看到产品中或产品中所含的化学物质，也不知道何时接触化学物质。我们无法摆脱这个问题。我们也无法以循环利用的方式解决这个问题。我们需要更根本的变化。
 这些化学物质现在是否已经根深蒂固在我们的环境中而无法倒计时？
 他们当然是根深蒂固的，但是他们并不是太根深蒂固。例如，我进行了两项妊娠队列研究。其中一个是在2000年至2002年之间招募的妇女。第二个是在10年后的2010年至2012年招募的。我们测量了这两个人群尿液中邻苯二甲酸盐的含量，包括危害最大的邻苯二甲酸盐。自2008年《消费者保护法》颁布以来，邻苯二甲酸盐的行为发生了许多变化，人们正在避免使用它们。我们在研究中看到的是，我们第二人口中女性的某些邻苯二甲酸盐减少了50％。很棒，对吧？坏消息是其他邻苯二甲酸酯取代了邻苯二甲酸酯。
 这种情况一遍又一遍地发生在化学药品中。它发生在阻燃剂中，发生在双酚中，发生在邻苯二甲酸盐中。发生这种情况是因为在将产品投放市场之前，我们不需要安全证明或缺乏荷尔蒙活动的证明。这就是令人遗憾的替代。顺便说一句，在欧洲，这种情况的发生率几乎不一样。在欧洲联盟中，有一项名为“化学品注册，评估，授权和限制”（REACH）的法规。根据REACH，制造商有义务在化学品投放市场和进入我们的身体之前证明其安全性。在美国，我们没有这项法律，我们是豚鼠。在我们更改法规之前，我们无法解决此问题。
  我们必须确保制造商在对我们的身体安全之前，先要了解化学品具有的特性。它们不应该具有荷尔蒙活性，尤其是在剂量很低的情况下。这很重要，因为我们发现在使用BPA时，这些法规可能会因为高剂量的测试而完全忽略了风险；低剂量的风险得到了正确的解决。因此，我们必须使用低剂量无害的化学物质。另外，它们也不能存储在环境中。我们必须确保它们不会进入我们的水，我们的鱼和我们的饲料中，并稍后通过这些不同的路线返回给我们。我们正在谈论化工企业的大修。
  绝对地。越来越多的运动被称为“绿色化学”。一些公司围绕这个想法形成了。他们对制造这些更安全的化学品，清洁我们的水并进行正确测试感兴趣。这将是昂贵的，并且将需要时间。但我认为这是可能的。例如，由可再生资源（例如土豆和玉米淀粉）制成的生物塑料是替代我们邻苯二甲酸盐的首次尝试。我认为这些塑料的碳足迹成本很高。但我认为，教训是，我们还有其他选择。如果有意愿，那就有可能发生。 
要让立法者和公众关注这些化学物质对我们生殖健康的影响，需要采取什么措施？
 人们关心自己的健康和即时健康。我认为，我们现在对人的健康产生的影响越多-肥胖增加，勃起功能障碍越多，性满意度降低，月经问题越多-他们就会越听。我认为这是我们必须向人们展示的东西。显示夫妻难以怀孕和体内物质之间的联系也很有说服力。而且，顺便说一句，您将无法停止将它们传递给您的孩子和后代。我们接连暴露自己，情况越来越糟。它是累积的。因此，至关重要的是我们必须迅速采取行动。
  我认为这是一个主要问题。例如，代孕率上升了。辅助繁殖的速度有所提高。越来越多地，我们将依靠这些技术解决方案来生孩子，而且我认为人们并不完全满意这种繁殖方式。但这是一些好消息。华盛顿州立大学的帕特里夏·亨特（Patricia Hunt）博士首先表明，当雄性小鼠在生命早期接触这些化学物质，然后使其后代接触，然后对其后代进行接触，则这些化学物质会随着时间的推移而积累。这些化学物质在那里，而且不会消失。但是她向我们证明，如果您带了一个男性，如果您暴露了这种疾病，并且停止暴露，则需要三到四代，但是他们将恢复生殖健康。因此，这确实令人鼓舞。令人沮丧的部分是花费的时间长度。
note_en:
Many men today have just half the number of sperm their grandfathers had. The  shocking discovery was published in 2017 by Shanna Swan, PhD, an environmental and reproductive epidemiologist at the Icahn School of Medicine at Mount Sinai in New York.
 After analyzing 185 studies involving nearly 45,000 healthy men, Swan and her team found that over the past four decades, sperm counts among men in Western countries had dropped by more than 50%.
 Why the huge decline? Swan  says many factors are at play — alcohol use, smoking, body weight, and a lack of exercise are a few. But she has zeroed in on another, more insidious cause: exposure to common chemicals that interfere with the body’s production of hormones.
 Swan has been studying these so-called endocrine disruptors for the past 30 years. And it turns out men’s sperm isn’t the only thing they affect: They may be changing human sexual development and reproduction in broader ways, too. In girls, exposure to such chemicals has been linked to earlier onset of puberty. Women, meanwhile, are experiencing a decline in egg quality and more miscarriages.
 In a new book,  Count Down: How Our Modern World Is Threatening Sperm Counts, Altering Male and Female Reproductive Development, and Imperiling the Future of the Human Race, Swan outlines the hidden damage these chemicals are having on our fertility and why we must act now to prevent them from causing further damage.
 In the book, she makes a provocative claim: Humans may not have the ability to reproduce naturally for much longer. She estimates  that by 2050, a large portion of the global population will need assisted reproductive technology to procreate. I talked to Swan to learn more about these chemical exposures and what can be done to avert an infertile future.
   Shanna Swan: We’re talking about hundreds, if not thousands — because most of them haven’t been tested — of chemicals that are in our daily lives. The endocrine disruptors that I have studied the most are the phthalates. It’s a big class of chemicals. Several of them make plastics soft and flexible. Others have the ability to promote absorption and retention. They’re put in personal care products. For instance, they help hand lotion go through your skin, and they help the smell get into your nose. They also help the pesticides get to the plant. They’re added to many, many products.
 The most common source of exposure to phthalates is food. That’s kind of surprising, but that’s because phthalates are in all soft tubing and soft coverings used to process and package food. Heat combined with food is a bad combination in the presence of phthalates. They have many impacts on humans, and the one that I am most concerned about is their ability to lower testosterone.
 Then there’s the bisphenols, which are compounds that act on estrogen, as opposed to the phthalates, which act on testosterone. They make plastics hard. They’re found in baby bottles, drinking water bottles, and other products. The most familiar is BPA but there are substitutes like BPS, BPF, and so on.
 Another class of chemicals that are very concerning are pesticides going all the way back to DDT and currently all the way  up to Roundup. These are hormonally active in many, many ways, depending on the particular formulation. They get into our foods. They’ve also been found in people who are handling these products occupationally. Pesticides are a major source of exposure and a real risk to our endocrine system.
 Then we have the flame retardants, which is a class of chemicals that has gotten a lot of attention over the years. They’ve changed, they’ve become “apparently” safer but never really safe. These are chemicals that are in everything from kids’ pajamas to firefighting foam to cushions on our sofas. They’re really hard to get rid of in our homes.
 Then there’s the class of chemicals  known as PFAS. Those are chemicals that are used, for example, in Teflon to make things stick-resistant. They also make things water-resistant, like our raincoats. These are chemicals that have strong hormonal effects, particularly on the thyroid and the immune system.
 You mentioned the effect of these chemicals on male fertility, but what about women’s fertility?
 That’s harder to study. Most of my work on specific hormonally active agents has been on men. But there are more and more studies coming out showing that they can also change the levels of hormone in a woman’s body. They can interfere with menstrual function, age at the first menstrual cycle, age at menopause, premature ovarian failure, and so on.
 Men are much better studied because, traditionally, men have always been better studied. Until recently, the National Institutes of Health didn’t even require you to study both sexes. Secondly, in men, it’s very easy to look at the organs and sperm. It’s harder to examine a woman’s eggs and her ovaries. You have to have an ultrasound to do that. So, women are harder to study for many reasons.
 Lifestyle factors, like diet and exercise, and sociological ones, like people waiting longer to have children, are linked to a decline in fertility too. How much can we blame on these chemicals?
 That’s a great question, and I don’t know the answer to that. I believe, based on my work, that it is substantial. But even the lifestyle factors that appear to be quite different from these chemicals are not really that different. For example, one lifestyle factor that affects fertility is obesity. A number of these chemicals are obesogens. They cause obesity, they interfere with the hormones that are related to satiation and metabolism. They make us more likely to get fat, and so obesity is on the pathway between exposure and infertility.
 Let’s take another example. In order to create a baby, you obviously have to have robust sexual function. We have quite a bit of evidence now that these endocrine-disrupting chemicals can interfere with sexual function. In  a study in China, men who manufactured BPA had more sexual problems and more erectile dysfunction. In  our study, we asked women how satisfied they were sexually, and women who had higher levels of some phthalates said they were less satisfied. So, you can see that there’s a lot of steps to making a baby and there are a lot of opportunities for chemicals to interfere with those all along the way. It may not be a direct effect, and that’s one of the reasons it’s hard to quantify.
 We know that the quality of women’s eggs declines as they get older, which affects fertility. Are chemicals like phthalates exacerbating this?
 The quality of a woman’s eggs can be affected by environmental chemicals. There’s a program, a research program at Harvard called the Environment and Reproductive Health (EARTH) Study. In that study, what they do is they take couples that are coming in for assisted reproduction, like IVF, and they get urine and blood samples from the man and the woman. They’ve shown in multiple papers that the quality of the egg and embryo is worse and there are fewer successful births when the mother and the father are exposed to a wide number of environmental chemicals. That speaks to the quality of the egg and sperm. They’re both important. They both have to be of high quality, and those both can be impaired through environmental chemicals.
 The explanation I hear a lot, and it’s very frustrating, is that people are choosing to have fewer children and the decline in fertility is because they’re choosing to delay childbearing. If it wasn’t for that, fertility would be just fine. Well, that’s actually not true because a lot of the fertility problems we’re seeing are in young women. We actually wrote a paper about that using the National Survey of Family Growth data. We showed that the problems in young women were actually higher than those in older ones. So, I don’t buy that the decline in fertility is solely due to delayed childbearing.
 Is avoiding endocrine-disrupting chemicals as simple as not buying BPA plastic or eating organic foods that weren’t grown with pesticides?
 It’s not that simple, although those things help. There have been studies where children and adults have eaten organic food, and they’ve shown a lot of positives. In  one study we did in young men, we found that when the men ate organic food, their semen quality was better. So, I think it definitely helps, but the hard thing is, most of our exposure is hidden. You can’t always see what chemicals are in or on products, you don’t know when you’re getting exposed. We can’t buy our way out of this problem. We also can’t recycle our way out of this problem. We need much more fundamental changes.
 Are these chemicals too entrenched in our environment at this point to be able to turn the clock back?
 They certainly are entrenched, but they’re not too entrenched. For example, I conducted two pregnancy cohort studies. In  one of them, the women were recruited between 2000 and 2002. In the  second one, they were recruited 10 years later in 2010 to 2012. We measured the levels of phthalates in their urine in both populations, including the most harmful phthalates. After the Consumer Protection Act of 2008, there was a lot of change in behavior about phthalates and people were avoiding them. What we saw in the study was that there was a 50% reduction in certain phthalates in the women in our second population. Fabulous, right? The bad news is that other phthalates came in to take their place.
 That happens over and over and over again with chemicals. It happened with the flame retardants, it happened with the bisphenols, it happened with the phthalates. It happens because we don’t require proof of safety or proof of lack of hormonal activity before we put a product into the market. It’s called regrettable substitution. It doesn’t happen to nearly the same extent in Europe, by the way. In the European Union, they have a legislation called Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals (REACH). Under REACH, the manufacturer is obligated to prove the safety of the chemical before it is put into the marketplace and into our bodies. Here in the United States, we don’t have that legislation and we are the guinea pigs. Until we change the regulations, we can’t fix this problem.
  We have to make sure that the manufacturers understand what properties chemicals have before they’re going to be safe for our bodies. They shouldn’t be hormonally active, and especially not at very low doses. That’s important because we saw with BPA, the regulations could totally miss the risks because they test at high doses; the risks from low doses got right through. So, we have to have chemicals that are not harmful at low doses. Also, they can’t be stored in the environment. We have to make sure that they’re not going into our water, into our fish, and into our feed and coming back to us later through those various routes. We’re talking about a major overhaul of the chemical enterprise.
  Absolutely. There’s a growing movement called “green chemistry.” Some companies have formed around this idea. They’re interested in making these safer chemicals and cleaning up our water and testing properly. It’s going to be expensive and it’s going to take time. But I think it’s possible. For example, bioplastics, which are made from renewable sources like potatoes and corn starch, were a first attempt at replacing our phthalates. I think it’s turning out to be costly in terms of the carbon footprint of these plastics. But I think the lesson is, we have alternatives. If the will is there, then it can happen.
 What’s it going to take to get lawmakers and the public to care about the effect of these chemicals on our reproductive health?
 People care about their own health, their immediate health. I think the more that we can show effects on people’s health right now — increased obesity, more erectile dysfunction, less sexual satisfaction, more menstrual problems — the more they’ll listen. I think that’s the kind of thing we’re going to have to show people. Showing the links between couples that are having trouble getting pregnant and what’s in their bodies is also persuasive. And, by the way, you’re not going to be able to stop passing them on to your children and later generations. We’re successively exposing ourselves and things get worse and worse. It’s cumulative. So, it’s really critical that we act quickly.
  I think it’s a major problem. For example, the rate of surrogacy has gone up. The rate of assisted reproduction has gone up. More and more, we’re leaning on those technological solutions to have children, and I don’t think people are completely happy with reproducing that way. But here’s some good news. Patricia Hunt, PhD, at Washington State University  has  shown, first of all, that when male mice are exposed early in life to these chemicals, and then their offspring are exposed, and then their offspring is exposed, the chemicals build up over time. The chemicals are out there and they’re not going away. But she’s shown that if you take a male who’s been exposed and you stop the exposure, it will take three to four generations, but they will be back to reproductive health. So that’s really encouraging. The discouraging part of it is the length of time that it takes.
title: 科学家警告说，人类可能无法自然繁殖更长的时间
title_en: Humans May Not Be Able to Reproduce Naturally Much Longer, Scientist Warns

 9To5Google发现了使您的电视哑巴的选项。在运行Google智能电视平台的电视的设置过程中，会提供多种功能，包括运行应用程序，接收内容推荐以及启用Google Assistant的功能。这是您期望电视提供的核心选项的同时：能够通过天线观看实况广播，并可以通过其HDMI端口访问所连接的设备。
 在以前的智能电视上，禁用智能功能的唯一方法是拒绝同意弹出的服务条款，而不输入您的WiFi密码。但是，Google决定提供一种更加用户友好的方法。在设置过程中，您可以选择“设置基本电视”。这样做是为了让您的电视接收实况广播并访问HDMI端口，但无其他操作。没有应用，没有Google Assistant，也没有内容推荐。您还可以选择返回到设置过程，并在需要时启用这些智能功能。
  为什么会有人想要这么笨的电视呢？原因之一是因为他们更喜欢通过连接的媒体流设备（例如Amazon Fire TV Stick，Apple TV或Roku Ultra）来管理对流服务，应用程序和Internet的访问。现在，我们需要的是一些运行Google平台的新电视。索尼和TCL的机型计划于今年推出，并且肯定会陆续推出。

     If you go out and purchase a new  TV today, it's going to have smart TV features allowing access to  streaming services and the internet. However, if that new TV is running on the Google TV platform, it's possible to easily disable all the smart features during setup.
 The option to make your TV dumb was spotted by  9To5Google. During the setup of a TV running Google's smart TV platform, multiple features are offered including the ability to run apps, receive content recommendations, and enable Google Assistant. That's alongside the core options you'd expect from a TV: the ability to watch live broadcasts through an aerial and having access to attached devices via its HDMI ports.
 On smart TVs I've setup before, the only way to disable smart features has been by declining to agree to the terms of service that pop-up and not entering your WiFi password. However, Google decided to offer a more user-friendly way of doing this. As part of the setup process you can select "Set up basic TV." What this does is allow your TV to receive live broadcasts and access the HDMI ports, but nothing else. There's no apps, no Google Assistant, and no content recommendations. You also have the option to go back into the setup process and enable these smart features whenever you like.
  Why would anyone want such a dumb TV? The number one reason will be because they prefer to manage their access to streaming services, apps, and the internet through an attached  media streaming device such as an  Amazon Fire TV Stick,  Apple TV, or  Roku Ultra. Now all we need is for some of these new TVs running Google's platform to launch. Models from Sony and TCL are planned for launch this year and more will surely follow.

一组科学家周四宣布，银河系中最大，最著名的黑洞之一比天文学家想象的还要大。这一发现为长期恒星在最终灭亡的过程中如何演化提供了模型。
 天鹅座X-1（看不见的X射线发射物体）和一颗名为HDE 226868的胖蓝色恒星每5.6天彼此环绕。天鹅座X-1是1964年发现的最早的X射线天文学源之一，当时天文学家开始将盖革（Geiger）宇宙计数器放到太空中，也是最早被认为是可能出现的黑洞之一。 X射线是由气体产生的，气体绕着宇宙引流旋转时被加热到数百万度。
 天鹅座X-1的质量最初估计是太阳的15倍，是银河系中已知的质量最大，发光度最高的X射线双星系统之一。
 现在新的测量将该数字增加到21个太阳质量。改头换面不会改变人们对宇宙的总体看法；天鹅座X-1仍然是一个黑洞，几乎是科幻小说中爱因斯坦在天体现实中的怪异现象。但是现在关于天鹅座X-1怎样成为黑洞的细节尚存疑问。
 芝加哥大学理论天体物理学家丹尼尔·霍尔兹（Daniel Holz）在一封电子邮件中写道：“这样一个经典的历史天文学资料的质量发生重大变化（至少对天文学家而言是个大问题）。” 。
 澳大利亚科廷大学国际射电天文学研究中心的詹姆斯·米勒·琼斯也通过电子邮件写道：“我们意识到，以现有的最佳估计，在银河系中形成的21太阳质量的黑洞实在太大，无法形成。恒星风中大质量恒星损失的质量。”
 Miller-Jones博士及其国际同事在《科学》杂志和《天体物理学杂志》上的两篇配套论文中报告了这一结果。 
正如其中一篇论文所述，天鹅座X-1的故事始于昏暗的过去，一对巨大的蓝色恒星相互环绕运行。两颗恒星中较大的一颗演化得更快，膨胀并开始向其伴星上溢出氢气。主恒星的剩余部分开始于其初盛时，其质量是太阳的55或75倍，但由于恒星核心不断燃烧，它在剧烈的恒星风中会流失更多的质量。最终，用尽的恒星用尽了所有的热核燃料，坍塌成了一个黑洞。
 有时，视情况而定，这种残局崩溃以惊人的超新星爆炸为标志。然而，在这种情况下，米勒·琼斯博士在一封电子邮件中写道：“我们认为黑洞是通过几乎直接坍缩成黑洞而不是II型超新星爆炸而形成的。”他说，这样的爆炸将把这对双星从具有类似质量的恒星聚集中踢出，并在其中形成并显然仍然存在。
 从那以后，黑洞一直在供气，从其膨胀的邻居中吸取天然气，据米勒-琼斯博士说，该天然气约有40个太阳质量。
 Cygnus X-1质量的新测量是偶然的。 Miller-Jones博士说：“我们尚未着手重新测量距离和黑洞质量。” “但是当我们分析数据后，我们就意识到了其全部潜力。”
 2016年春天，米勒·琼斯博士和他的小组在国家射电天文台的甚长基线阵列上观测了天鹅座X-1，该阵列是在新墨西哥州Socorro以外运行的全国性天线网络。流入黑洞的发射X射线的气体与射出黑洞的高速无线电射流之间的连接。
 但是，该过程的一部分使他们能够对距天鹅座X-1的距离进行三角测量，将距离从大约6,000光年增加到7,000多光年。有趣的是，米勒-琼斯博士指出，这也使距离与欧洲航天局盖亚太空望远镜的早期结果更好地吻合，该望远镜的测量在先前接受的距离下处于轻微的张力下。
 将距离的变化计入光度和质量的计算中，黑洞的估计质量增加了约40％，达到21个太阳质量。 
Miller-Jones博士说，这令人兴奋，但是直到他与莫纳什大学的理论同事Ilya Mandel交谈后，他才意识到他们所做的一切的全部含义。
  对质量的新估计使天鹅座X-1处于一种神奇的阈值之上。天文学家知道银河系及其附近有数十个黑洞X射线双星系统，它们的推算质量都不到太阳的20倍。这个明显的极限表明，至少从恒星的坍塌开始，黑洞很难变得更大。
 但是自2016年以来，激光干涉仪引力波天文台（LIGO）和处女座天线一直在记录太空中黑洞的碰撞，其中许多黑洞的质量远远超过20个太阳质量。
 霍尔茨博士写道：“当最初的GW探测结果是由两个黑洞组成的双星时，每个黑洞约有30个太阳质量，这给整个社区中的许多人带来了深远的震惊。”团队研究这些结果。
 矛盾是显而易见的。 LIGO结果表明，总的来说，黑洞比X射线结果表明的要大。关于恒星演化的大部分假设来自想象宇宙风的细节，当这些恒星喷出并变成黑洞时，它们会将质量从垂死的恒星上剥离掉。新的结果表明，天文学家需要减少对恒星如何失去质量的计算。
 Miller-Jones博士说：“已经向上修正了天鹅座X-1中黑洞的质量，我们意识到，为了解释我们的测量结果，我们需要向下修正大质量恒星的质量损失率。这是促使我们撰写本文的关键见解，展示了科学合作的力量，将各种技能结合在一起以解决一个有趣的问题。”
 霍尔茨博士说，他并不担心代表天文学家。他指出，恒星演化的天体物理学非常复杂，提供了许多旋钮来进行计算，以使结果有意义。他说：“关于制造如此大的黑洞，我的猜测是恒星建模者将能够轻松容纳它。” “他们是一群很有创造力的人！”
note_en:
One of the biggest and first known black holes in the Milky Way galaxy is more massive than astronomers thought, a team of scientists announced on Thursday. The finding throws a wrench into long-held models of how massive stars evolve on the way to the ultimate doom.
 Cygnus X-1, an unseen, X-ray-emitting object, and a fat blue star called HDE 226868 circle each other every 5.6 days. Cygnus X-1 was one of the earliest celestial sources of X-rays ever discovered, in 1964, when astronomers began lofting cosmic Geiger counters into space, and one of the first to be considered as a possible black hole. The X-rays are produced by gas that is heated to millions of degrees as it swirls around the cosmic drain.
 With a mass originally estimated at 15 times that of the sun, Cygnus X-1 is one of the most massive and most luminous of the X-ray binary systems known in the Milky Way.
 New measurements have now raised that figure to 21 solar masses. The makeover does not change the overall perception of the cosmos; Cygnus X-1 is still a black hole, an almost science-fictional manifestation of Einsteinian weirdness in celestial reality. But the details of how Cygnus X-1 became a black hole are now in doubt.
 “A significant change in the mass of such a classic and historical astronomical source is a big deal (at least to astronomers),” Daniel Holz, a theoretical astrophysicist at the University of Chicago who was not part of the study, wrote in an email.
 Also by email, James Miller-Jones of the International Center for Radio Astronomy Research at Curtin University in Australia wrote: “We realized that a 21-solar-mass black hole was too massive to form in the Milky Way with the best existing estimates of the amount of mass lost by massive stars in stellar winds.”
 Dr. Miller-Jones and an international cast of colleagues reported the result  in the journal Science and in a pair of companion papers in Astrophysical Journal.
  As one of the papers recounts, the story of Cygnus X-1 starts in the dim past with a pair of massive blue stars orbiting each other. The bigger of the two stars evolved faster, expanded and began spilling hydrogen gas onto its companion star. What remained of the primary star, which started out in its prime with a mass of 55 or 75 times that of the sun, shed more of its mass in fierce stellar winds as its core kept burning. Finally, having exhausted all of its thermonuclear fuel, the spent star collapsed into a black hole.
 Sometimes, depending on circumstance, this endgame collapse is marked by a  stupendous supernova explosion. In this case, however, Dr. Miller-Jones wrote in an email, “We think that the black hole formed via almost direct collapse into a black hole, rather than in a type II supernova explosion.” Such an explosion, he said, would have kicked the binary star pair out of an assemblage of similarly massive stars in which it formed and, apparently, still lives.
 Since then, the black hole has been feeding, pulling in gas from its puffed-up neighbor, which, with roughly 40 solar masses, has a lot to give, according to Dr. Miller-Jones.
 The new measurement of the mass of Cygnus X-1 was serendipitous. “We had not set out to remeasure the distance and black-hole mass,” Dr. Miller-Jones said. “But when we had analyzed our data, we realized its full potential.”
 In the spring of 2016, Dr. Miller-Jones and his group spent six days observing Cygnus X-1 with the National Radio Astronomy Observatory’s Very Long Baseline Array, a nationwide network of antennas operated out of Socorro, N.M. They were trying to investigate the connection between X-ray-emitting gas flowing into the black hole and high-speed radio jets shooting out of it.
 But part of the process allowed them to triangulate the distance to Cygnus X-1, increasing it from about 6,000 light-years to a little over 7,000. Interestingly, Dr. Miller-Jones noted, this also brought the distance into better agreement with early results from the European Space Agency’s Gaia space telescope, whose measurements had been in mild tension with the previously accepted distance.
 When that change in distance was factored into the calculations of luminosity and mass, the black hole’s estimated mass grew by about 40 percent, to 21 solar masses.
 That was exciting, Dr. Miller-Jones said, but it was not until he talked to a theoretical colleague, Ilya Mandel of Monash University, that he appreciated the full implications of what they had done.
  The new estimate of mass put Cygnus X-1 above a kind of magic threshold. Astronomers know of a few dozen black hole X-ray binary systems in the Milky Way and nearby, all of which have imputed masses of less than 20 times that of the sun. That apparent limit suggested that it was hard for black holes to grow more massive, at least from the collapse of stars.
 But since 2016, the Laser Interferometer Gravitational-Wave Observatory, or LIGO, and Virgo antennas have been recording the  collisions of black holes far out in space, many of them much larger than 20 solar masses.
 “When the very first GW detection turned out to be a binary composed of two black holes, each of approximately 30 solar masses, it came as a profound shock to many in the community,” wrote Dr. Holz, who is part of a large team studying those results.
 The contradiction was glaring. The LIGO results suggested that, in general, black holes were more massive than the X-ray results suggested. Much of what is assumed about stellar evolution comes from imagining the details of the cosmic winds that strip mass from dying stars as they sputter out and become black holes. The new results suggest that astronomers need to pare back their calculations of how stars lose their mass.
 “Having revised the mass of the black hole in Cygnus X-1 upward,” Dr. Miller-Jones said, “we realized that we would need to revise downward the mass-loss rate of massive stars in order to explain our measurements. This was the key insight that led us to write this paper, demonstrating the power of scientific collaboration, bringing a diverse range of skills together to attack an interesting problem.”
 Dr. Holz said he was not worried on behalf of the astronomers. The astrophysics of stellar evolution is very complicated, he noted, offering many knobs to turn in the calculations to help the results make sense. “As for making a black hole of this mass, my guess is that stellar modelers will be able to accommodate it without too much trouble,” he said. “They are a very creative bunch!”
title: 著名的黑洞得到更新
title_en: A Famous Black Hole Gets an Update

阿姆斯特丹（路透社）-荷兰议会周四通过了一项不具约束力的动议，称对中国维吾尔族穆斯林的对待等于种族灭绝，这是欧洲国家的首例此类行径。
  活动人士和联合国维权专家说，至少有100万穆斯林被拘留在偏远的新疆西部地区的难民营中。活动人士和一些西方政客指责中国使用酷刑，强迫劳动和绝育措施。
 中国否认新疆有任何侵犯人权的行为，并说其营地提供职业培训，是打击极端主义所必需的。
 荷兰动议说：“中国正在发生对维吾尔族的种族灭绝事件，”没有直接说中国政府应对此负责。
 中国驻海牙大使馆周四表示，关于在新疆发生种族灭绝的任何建议都是“彻头彻尾的谎言”，荷兰议会“故意污蔑中国，严重干涉了中国的内政”。
  加拿大本周早些时候通过了一项决议，将中国对维吾尔族的种族灭绝的待遇标记为标签。
 荷兰动议说，中国政府的行动，例如“旨在防止生育的措施”和“有惩罚营”，属于联合国第260号决议（通常被称为种族灭绝公约）。 
外交大臣史蒂夫·布洛克（Stef Blok）说，政府不希望使用种族灭绝一词，因为联合国或国际法院尚未宣布这种情况。
 动议通过后，布洛克对记者说：“维吾尔人的处境令人极为关切。”他补充说，荷兰希望在此问题上与其他国家合作。
 议案的作者，中左翼D-66政党议员Sjoerd Sjoerdsma，已单独提议游说国际奥委会，将2022年冬季奥运会从北京撤离。
 他在对问题的电子邮件回复中告诉路透社：“认识到针对中国维吾尔族人的暴行是种族灭绝，它阻止了世界其他方向，迫使我们采取行动。”
  中国驻海牙大使馆在其网站上的声明中说，近年来，新疆维吾尔族人口一直在增长，生活水平更高，预期寿命更长。
 “你怎么称这为种族灭绝？”它说。 “与新疆有关的问题绝与人权，种族或宗教无关，而与打击暴力恐怖主义和继承有关。”
 中国驻日内瓦联合国大使周三指责西方列强利用维吾尔族问题来干涉该国的内政。
note_en:
AMSTERDAM (Reuters) - The Dutch parliament on Thursday passed a non-binding motion saying the treatment of the Uighur Muslim minority in China amounts to genocide, the first such move by a European country.
  Activists and U.N. rights experts say at least one million Muslims are being detained in camps in the remote western region of Xinjiang. The activists and some Western politicians accuse China of using torture, forced labour and sterilisations.
 China denies any human rights abuses in Xinjiang and says its camps provide vocational training and are needed to fight extremism.
 “A genocide on the Uighur minority is occurring in China,” the Dutch motion said, stopping short of directly saying that the Chinese government was responsible.
 The Chinese Embassy in The Hague said on Thursday any suggestion of a genocide in Xinjiang was an “outright lie” and the Dutch parliament had “deliberately smeared China and grossly interfered in China’s internal affairs.”
  Canada passed a resolution labelling China’s treatment of the Uighurs genocide earlier this week.
 The Dutch motion said that actions by the Chinese government such as “measures intended to prevent births” and “having punishment camps” fell under United Nations Resolution 260, generally known as the genocide convention.
    Foreign Minister Stef Blok said the government did not want to use the term genocide, as the situation has not been declared as such by the United Nations or by an international court.
 “The situation of the Uighurs is a cause of great concern”, Blok told reporters after the motion was passed, adding that the Netherlands hoped to work with other nations on the matter.
 The author of the motion, lawmaker Sjoerd Sjoerdsma of the centre-left D-66 Party, has separately proposed lobbying the International Olympic Committee to move the 2022 Winter Olympics away from Beijing.
 “Recognising the atrocities that are taking place against the Uighurs in China for what they are, namely genocide, prevents the world from looking the other way and forces us into action,” he told Reuters in an emailed response to questions.
  In a statement on its website, the Chinese Embassy in The Hague said the Uighur population in Xinjiang has been growing in in recent years, enjoying a higher standard of living, and a longer life expectancy.
 “How can you call this a genocide?” it said. “Xingjiang-related issues are never about human rights, ethnicity or religion, but about combating violent terrorism and succession.”
 China’s ambassador to the United Nations in Geneva accused Western powers on Wednesday of using the Uighur issue to meddle in his country’s internal affairs.
title: 荷兰议会：中国对维吾尔族的待遇是种族灭绝
title_en: Dutch parliament: China's treatment of Uighurs is genocide
